---
title: "Data Science"
description: "Turn questions and datasets into cleaned, analyzed, visualized, and communicated results."
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this chapter matters

Turn questions and datasets into cleaned, analyzed, visualized, and communicated results.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.questions}
### Questions

- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Workflow and Loading Data](workflow-and-loading.qmd) — Move from question to dataset and load tabular data with pandas.
- [Inspecting, Selecting, and Cleaning](inspecting-selecting-cleaning.qmd) — Understand DataFrames, choose relevant data, and handle missing values.
- [Grouping and Statistics](grouping-and-statistics.qmd) — Summarize categories and describe distributions.
- [Visualization with seaborn](visualization-with-seaborn.qmd) — Create statistical plots with seaborn.
- [Communicating Results](communicating-results.qmd) — Explain findings with evidence, uncertainty, and limitations.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "data-science-overview",
  "title": "Data Science overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Data Science chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Turn questions and datasets into cleaned, analyzed, visualized, and communicated results.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Turn questions and datasets into cleaned, analyzed, visualized, and communicated results."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
